# Scope Control Checklist: SPEC-001 Foundation Migrations

**Purpose**: Validate whether the SPEC-001 requirements and plan keep the phase limited to migration-only schema work and block drift into SPEC-002 or SPEC-005 runtime scope.
**Created**: 2026-04-25
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` workflow based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Do the requirements clearly confine SPEC-001 to additive migrations, rollback SQL, rollback procedure docs, and migration smoke verification rather than runtime behavior or product-surface work? [Completeness, Spec section User Story 4, Spec section FR-014, Plan section Summary]
- [x] CHK002 Are the no-SQL safety gates for `ready_for_owner` and Sandbox terminology documented as proofs only, not as implementation work? [Completeness, Spec section Clarifications, Spec section FR-004, Spec section FR-007, Spec section FR-014]
- [x] CHK003 Is storage-only feature-flag scope explicit enough that implementers cannot mistake `workspaces.feature_flags` persistence for SPEC-002 runtime flag resolution? [Completeness, Spec section Clarifications, Spec section FR-008, Spec section FR-014]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK004 Is notification work explicitly excluded alongside UI/config/type/Zod/GitHub-label/Kanban/runtime feature-flag behavior, rather than left implicit? [Clarity, Spec section User Story 4, Spec section FR-014, Workflow Phase 4 Scope Control Prompt]
- [x] CHK005 Is the `ready_for_owner` boundary specific enough to keep application vocabulary, labels, Kanban, and related runtime behavior in SPEC-005 instead of SPEC-001? [Clarity, Spec section Clarifications, Spec section FR-007, Spec section FR-014, Roadmap Phase 4]
- [x] CHK006 Is the Sandbox terminology boundary specific enough to preserve `agents.workspace_path`, forbid `sandbox_path`, and avoid runtime/UI/type cleanup drift into this spec? [Clarity, Spec section Clarifications, Spec section FR-004, Spec section FR-014, Roadmap Phase 0]
- [x] CHK007 Is Strict Scope stated unambiguously as `N/A` unless an unavoidable new TS/TSX production module is introduced and justified? [Clarity, Spec section Clarifications, Plan section Technical Context, Plan section Constitution Check]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK008 Do the spec and plan align on keeping TypeScript/Zod/runtime behavior out of scope while preserving the live schema names and DB-status contract? [Consistency, Spec section FR-004, Spec section FR-007, Spec section FR-014, Plan section Technical Context, Plan section Constitution Check]
- [x] CHK009 Do the plan constraints explicitly guard future `tasks.md` generation from drifting into SPEC-002 `resolveFlag()` or Sandbox cleanup work and SPEC-005 `ready_for_owner` runtime work? [Consistency, Plan section Technical Context, Workflow Phase 4 Scope Control Prompt, Workflow Phase 5/6 dependency notes]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK010 Are downstream-task drift scenarios covered clearly enough that reviewers can reject migration plans which add TS/TSX strict-scope files, runtime feature-flag behavior, or Phase 4 vocabulary work? [Coverage, Spec section User Story 4, Plan section Constraints, Workflow Phase 6]

## Notes

- Initial checklist pass identified two wording gaps: the spec needed explicit notification exclusion to match the workflow scope prompt, and the plan needed an explicit Phase 5 task-generation guard against SPEC-002 and SPEC-005 runtime drift.
